title: thymus fallax var. hayderensis
intro: Thymus fallax var. hayderensis, also known as Hayder's thyme, is a type of flowering plant in the mint family, characterized by its small, pinkish-white flowers and grayish-green foliage. It is primarily used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which make it useful in treating various infections and wounds. The health benefits of Hayder's thyme include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and boost the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Hayder's thyme include teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
